title: carlina libanotica
intro: Carlina libanotica, also known as the Syrian scorpion grass or Levant hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Carlina libanotica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and even act as an anthelmintic to treat parasitic worms. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
